title: Cinema Camera Controls & Enhanced Blur Intensity

requirements.text: Redesign the camera viewfinder control layout to adopt a cinema camera aesthetic: replace the current controls with a professional cinematography-inspired UI featuring an anamorphic widescreen letterbox overlay toggle (2.39:1 crop guides), a cinema-style circular exposure wheel/dial for EV adjustment, cine color profile selector (Rec.709, Log-C flat, D-Cinema), and a focal length/lens indicator overlay label. Group these controls in a dedicated 'Cinema Controls' panel that slides up when Cinematic or Video mode is active. Keep all existing functional hooks (useManualControls, useCinematicMode, etc.) wired to the new UI elements.

quotes: I need cinema camera like controls

acceptanceCriteria: A circular exposure wheel/dial is visible and functional in Cinematic and Video modes, replacing or augmenting the existing EV slider
A cine color profile selector with at least Rec.709, Log-C (flat), and D-Cinema options is visible and applies a CSS/canvas filter to the live preview
An anamorphic crop guide toggle (2.39:1 letterbox bars) is accessible and renders as a semi-transparent overlay on the viewfinder
A focal length/lens indicator label (e.g. '24mm eq.') is shown as a HUD overlay on the viewfinder
All new controls use the existing dark amber/gold theme and glass-morphism styling consistent with the app's design tokens
Existing functionality (zoom, torch, grid, timer, manual controls) remains fully operational

requirements.text: Increase the background blur intensity across all blur-producing effects: in useAIBlur, raise the canvas blur filter sigma so the background blur is visibly stronger (minimum effective blur radius equivalent to CSS blur(24px) or higher); in useCinematicMode, increase the background blur sigma applied to the off-screen canvas to a minimum of blur(28px) equivalent; in FocusEditPanel, raise the default and maximum blur intensity slider values so the bokeh mask produces a more pronounced separation. Expose a 'Blur Strength' quick-toggle button in the camera controls row (Light / Strong / Max) that scales the blur multiplier at runtime across all active blur effects.
acceptanceCriteria: AI Blur background is visibly much stronger — background details are heavily obscured at default strength
Cinematic Mode background blur is stronger by default, producing clear subject/background separation
FocusEditPanel maximum blur intensity is increased so exported frames show pronounced bokeh
A 'Blur Strength' toggle button with three levels (Light, Strong, Max) appears in the camera controls row when AI Blur or Cinematic mode is active
Selecting 'Max' produces near-complete background obscuration while keeping the subject mask sharp
The blur strength preference persists in localStorage across sessions

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor
Reuse existing hooks (useAIBlur, useCinematicMode, useManualControls, useCinematicMode) — extend them rather than replacing them
New UI controls must use existing Tailwind design tokens and amber/gold accent colors from the established theme
nonGoals: Adding new backend endpoints or data models
Changing the gallery page layout or lightbox behavior
Implementing server-side color science or LUT processing
Adding new camera modes beyond those already in the ModeSelector
